text: Planning a trip is always exciting. Last summer, I traveled to Japan with a detailed itinerary. Experiencing a new culture was fascinating, from the quiet temples to the busy city streets. The local food was a highlight—I tried sushi and ramen that tasted completely different from what I eat at home. Language barriers were sometimes challenging; I often used hand gestures and simple phrases to communicate. Despite the difficulties, struggling to order meals or ask for directions created funny moments. Now, looking back, those memories are the most precious part of the journey. Travel teaches you that mistakes can become wonderful stories.
